---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
    toc: false
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)
library(reactablefmtr)

source("R/utils.R")
```

```{r}
# set project path for ad hoc request folder where we'll store data for the updated analysis
sp_data_site_updates_path <- csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data/website_updates")

state_name <- "United States"
state_name_text <- "the United States"

srs_state_new <- read_rds(csg_sp_path("jr_data_library", "data", "analysis", "fbi", "srs", "fbi_srs_state.rds")) |> 
  mutate(crime_cat = if_else(group %in% c("Aggravated assault", "Homicide", "Rape", "Robbery"), "Violent", "Property")) |> 
  select(-state_fips, -group_cat, -starts_with("rate")) |> 
  filter(indicator != "Unsolved rate") |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = indicator, values_from = n) |> 
  janitor::clean_names() |> 
  select(
    year, state_name, state_abbr, group, crime_cat, pop_total, pop_adult,
    incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved, incidents_cleared) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_adult = incidents_reported / pop_adult
  )

srs_by_cat_us <- srs_state_new |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2014) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total / 4,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_us <- srs_state_new |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2014) |>
  group_by(year, group) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    incidents_solved = incidents_reported - incidents_unsolved,
    pct_solved = incidents_solved / incidents_reported
  )

### import state-level file for mapping
### aggregate counts and create rates
### only need 2019 and 2024 (to calculate change between the two)
### keeping values from each year for tooltips
srs_state_to_map <- srs_state_new |>
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year %in% c(2019, 2024)) |>
  group_by(state_name, state_abbr, pop_total, year) |>
  summarize(
    n_reported = sum(incidents_reported, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_cleared = sum(incidents_cleared, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_unsolved = sum(incidents_unsolved, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ### calculate rates
  mutate(
    solved_rate = n_cleared / n_reported,
    # unsolved_rate = n_unsolved / n_reported,
    viol_crime_rate_100k = (n_reported / pop_total) * 100000
  ) |>
  group_by(state_name) |>
  ### currently multiplying rate by 100 instead of using scales::percent
  ### may be better way to format within hc
  ### calculate change over time
  mutate(
    n_reported_2019 = n_reported[year == 2019],
    n_reported_2024 = n_reported[year == 2024],
    n_solved_2019 = n_cleared[year == 2019],
    n_solved_2024 = n_cleared[year == 2024],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2019 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019],
    viol_crime_rate_100k_2024 = viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2024],
    solved_rate_2019 = solved_rate[year == 2019] * 100,
    solved_rate_2024 = solved_rate[year == 2024] * 100,
    solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024 = solved_rate_2024 - solved_rate_2019,
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024 = ((viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                        2024] - viol_crime_rate_100k[year == 2019]) / viol_crime_rate_100k[year ==
                                                                                                                                             2019]
    ) * 100
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ### now drop 2019
  ### and keep population from 2024
  filter(year == 2024)


min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_us |>
  filter(year %in% c(min(year), max(year))) |>
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1]

pct_change <-
  (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]

change_text <- case_when(
  pct_change > 0.005  ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " more"),
  pct_change < -0.005 ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                               " fewer"),
  TRUE                ~ "nearly the same number of"
)

pct_change_solved <-
  min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[1]

change_text_solved <- case_when(
  pct_change_solved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_solved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_solved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point higher than"),
  pct_change_solved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_solved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points higher than"),
  pct_change_solved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_solved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
      pct_change_solved * 100
    ), 1),
    " percentage point lower than"),
  pct_change_solved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(
    pct_change_solved * 100
  ), 1),
  " percentage points lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
)

max_viol_crime_off <- srs_us |>
  filter(year == max(year)) |>
  arrange(desc(incidents_reported)) |>
  mutate(pct = incidents_reported / sum(incidents_reported))

### import and prep hex map geo
hex <- sf::read_sf("us_states_hexgrid.geojson") |>
  select(state_abb = iso3166_2)

hex_gj <- hex |>
  sf::st_transform(3857) |>
  geojsonsf::sf_geojson() |>
  jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE)

axis_expansion_mult <- 0.5
```

```{r }
### import df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024 for second table
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024 <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_viol_crime_unsolved_by_state_and_type.rds"))

### import asslgf_clean_national for lea expenditure analysis
asslgf_clean_national <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_national_level_police_expenditure_inflation_adjusted.rds")) |> 
  filter(year>=2013) 

### import ucr_employee_data_national_clean for officer workload analysis
ucr_employee_data_national_clean <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"website_update_lea_staffing_workload_national.rds")) |> 
  filter(year>=2013) 

```


```{r }
### now import all supplemental homicide dfs for visualization
### these files were cleaned and created here: R/12-prep-files-for-website-updates-2024.qmd

### shr_1976_2024_national_overall
shr_1976_2024_national_overall <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_overall_clear.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_relationship
shr_1976_2024_national_relationship <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_victim_offender_relationship.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_single_multiple_victims
shr_1976_2024_national_single_multiple_victims <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_single_multiple_victims.rds")) 


### shr_1976_2024_national_weapon
shr_1976_2024_national_weapon <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_weapon.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_circumstance
shr_1976_2024_national_circumstance <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_incident_circumstance.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_race
shr_1976_2024_national_race <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_victim_race_eth.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_gender
shr_1976_2024_national_gender <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_victim_gender.rds")) 

# ### shr_1976_2024_national_race_sex
# shr_1976_2024_national_race_sex <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_victim_race_gender.rds")) 

### shr_1976_2024_national_age
shr_1976_2024_national_age <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_site_updates_path,"shr_1976_2024_national_victim_age.rds")) 

```


# Trends in Violent Crime and Accountability

## 50-state violent crime data

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2019–2024 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

# Assign colors
darkorange  <- "#7B3014"
orange      <- "#D25E2D"
lightorange <- "#EDB799"
white       <- "#FFFFFF"
lightblue   <- "#9ed4ef"
regblue     <- "#007392"
darkblue    <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_2 <- srs_state_to_map |>
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean = as.numeric(comma(
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024, 1
    )),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in violent crime rate: ",
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean,
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2024): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2024, 1),
      "<br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k (2019): ",
      comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2019, 1)
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr))
  )

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_2$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_2,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in violent crime, 2019–2024",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#005FAD",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  ) |>
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Percent change in crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      c(
        darkblue,
        regblue,
        lightblue,
        white,
        lightorange,
        orange,
        darkorange
      )
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}%",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                  fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>  
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

## 2024 crime rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### choose blues from csgjcr for now
blue1 <- "#d4e9f8"
blue2 <- "#9ed4ef"
blue4 <- "#007392"
blue5 <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_1 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean = as.numeric(comma(viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024,1)),
    viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean = sprintf("%+3d%%", viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024_clean),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2024", "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(viol_crime_rate_100k_2024, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2024, 1), "<br>"
      ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr))
    )

### highchart 
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_1,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "viol_crime_rate_100k_2024",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
        )
      ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, violent crime rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
    ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Violent crime rate, 2024",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |> 
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                                 color = "#666666")
  ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1, blue2, blue4, blue5)),
    labels = list(
      style = list(fontSize = "12px", fontFamily = default_fonts),
      format = "{value:,.0f}"
      )
    ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 250,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
    ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
  hc_caption(
    text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
    align = "right",
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

:::

From 2019 to 2024, the national violent crime rate declined despite an increase in 2020. In 2024, rates of homicide, rape, and robbery were lower than their rates in 2019. The rate of aggravated assault was slightly higher in 2024 than in 2019, but down from its peak in 2020.

Trends in violent crime also varied by state. Between 2019 and 2024, violent crime rates in 39 states and the District of Columbia decreased, while violent crime rates in 11 states increased.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2019–2024 change

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### create object with states to exclude
states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz <- c("Illinois")

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_4 <- srs_state_to_map |>
   ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  mutate(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
     solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024_clean = as.numeric(comma(solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024,1)),
    solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024_clean = sprintf("%+3d", solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024_clean),
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, "</b><br>",
      "Change in solve rate: ",
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024_clean, "<br>",
      "Solve rate (2024): ", comma(solved_rate_2024, 1), "%", "<br>",
      "Solve rate (2019): ", comma(solved_rate_2019, 1), "%", "<br>"
    ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on solved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr)))
  

# Get data range
data_range <-
  range(srs_state_to_map_4$solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024,
        na.rm = TRUE)
min <- data_range[1]
max <- data_range[2]
# print(min); print(max)

# Adjust extremes so they are balanced at zero
new_min <- min(-abs(min), -abs(max))
new_max <- max(abs(min), abs(max))
# print(new_min); print(new_max)

### highchart
highchart() |>
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_4,
    joinBy = c("state_abb", "state_abbr"),
    value = "solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      format = "{point.state_abb}",
      style = list(
        fontSize = "13px",
        fontFamily = default_fonts,
        fontWeight = 700,
        textOutline = 0
      )
    ),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, percent point change: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Change in percentage of violent crime solved, 2019–2024",
    align = "left",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = header_font,
      color = "#005FAD",
      fontSize = "24px",
      fontWeight = "bold"
    )
  )  |> 
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage point change in rate of solved violent crimes from 2019 to 2024",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |>
  hc_colorAxis(
    min = new_min,
    max = new_max,
    endOnTick = TRUE,
    startOnTick = TRUE,
    stops = color_stops(
      7,
      rev(c(darkblue, regblue, lightblue, white, lightorange, orange, darkorange))
    ),
    labels = list(
      format = "{value}",
      style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                   fontFamily = default_fonts)
    )
  ) |>
  hc_legend(
    align = "center",
    horizontalAlign = "bottom",
    layout = "horizontal",
    # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
    symbolHeight = 25,
    symbolWidth = 300,
    x = 0,
    y = 0
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
  )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

## 2024 solve rate

```{r}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### add tooltip
srs_state_to_map_3 <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  ### filter out states in states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  # filter(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz) |> 
  mutate(solved_rate_2024 = ifelse(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                                     NA,solved_rate_2024)) |> 
  ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(
    tooltip = ifelse(!state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                     paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – 2024", "</b><br>",
      "Solve rate: ", comma(solved_rate_2024, 1),"%", "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2024, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(n_solved_2024, 1)
      ),
      paste0(
      state_name, " did not report enough data", "<br>",
      "on solved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates."
      )
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abbr)))

### highchart 
highchart() |> 
  hc_add_series_map(
    map = hex_gj,
    df = srs_state_to_map_3,
    joinBy = c("state_abb","state_abbr"),
    value = "solved_rate_2024",
    nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
    nullInteraction = TRUE,
    dataLabels = list(enabled = TRUE, format = "{point.state_abb}",
                        style = list(fontSize = "13px",
                                     fontFamily = default_fonts,
                                     fontWeight = 700,
                                     textOutline = 0)),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "state: {point.state_name}, solve rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )) |>
  hc_title(
    text = "Solved violent crime, 2024",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |>   
  hc_subtitle(
        text = "Percentage of violent crimes solved",
        align = "left",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts, fontSize = "16px",
                     color = "#666666")
    ) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(endOnTick = TRUE,
               startOnTick = TRUE,
               stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1,
                                        blue2,
                                        blue4,
                                        blue5)),
               labels = list(format = "{value}%",
                             style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                                          fontFamily = default_fonts))
                             ) |>
  hc_legend(align = "center",
            horizontalAlign = "bottom",
            layout = "horizontal",
            # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
            symbolHeight = 25,
            symbolWidth = 400,
            x = 0,
            y = 0) |> 
     hc_tooltip(
    style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
    formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")
    )  |>
   hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
      accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)),
      point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){window.top.location.href = this.options.url}")))
      )
    ) |>
    hc_caption(
        text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer",
        align = "right",
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts)
    ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

:::

Between 2019 and 2024, the percentage of violent crimes solved by law enforcement decreased in 26 states and the District of Columbia, while increasing in 24 states.

Illinois did not report enough data to the FBI on whether crimes were solved to make reliable estimates.

## Trends in reported violent crime

In `r max(srs_by_cat_us$year)`, `r srs_by_cat_us |> filter(year == max(year)) |> pull(incidents_reported_rate_total) |> comma(scale = 1e5)` violent crime incidents per 100,000 residents were reported to police in the United States.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_us |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```


```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year) |> 
  mutate(tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide") |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 0.1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      )
    )

rng <- range(to_plot_2$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_2$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

rng <- range(to_plot_3$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_3$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

rng <- range(to_plot_5$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_5 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_5$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

rng <- range(to_plot_4$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_4 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_4$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>  
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_legend(reversed = "true") |> 
  hc_setup()
```

:::


## Trends in solved violent crime

In `r max(srs_by_cat_us$year)`, `r srs_by_cat_us |> filter(year == max(year)) |> pull(pct_solved) |> percent(suffix = " percent")` of violent crime incidents reported to police in the United States were solved. The percentage of violent crimes that law enforcement solved fell from 2019 to 2022 and increased in 2024 to its highest level since 2015.

```{r}
solved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_solved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_solved = pct_solved * 100,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Solve rate: ", percent(pct_solved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(incidents_solved, 1)
      )
    )


rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult
y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, solve rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Solve rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_solved = pct_solved * 100,
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_solved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Solve rate: ", percent(pct_solved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents solved: ", comma(incidents_solved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_solved)

# rng <- range(to_plot$pct_solved)
# mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult
# 
# y_min <- rng[1] - mult
# y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
# y_max <- rng[2] + mult
# y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_solved, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, solve rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Solve rate of violent crime in ", state_name_text, " by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Crime Data Explorer") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

:::

## Violent crime and solved violent crime by state, 2024

The following tables include violent crime rates and solve rates by state and offense. For more information on violent crime solve rates for each offense by state, [visit state-specific pages here](https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/).  

::: {.panel-tabset}

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### Overall rates")
```

```{r}

### create state-level table
srs_state_to_map |> 
    select(
      state_name,
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2024,
      n_reported_2024,
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024,
      solved_rate_2024,
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024
      ) |> 
   ### recode all solved incident data to NA if state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  mutate(
    across(solved_rate_2024:solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024,
                ~ if_else(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                          NA, .)
           )
    ) |> 
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("state_name" = "asc"),
    columns = list(
      state_name = colDef(name = "State"),
      viol_crime_rate_100k_2024 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents per 100k (2024)"), 
      n_reported_2024 = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents Reported (2024)"), 
      viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2024 = colDef(
        name = "Percent change in violent crime rate (2019 to 2024)",
        cell = add_plus_sign_percent_change
        ),
      solved_rate_2024 = colDef(
        name = "Solve Rate (2024)",
        format = colFormat(suffix = "%", digits = 0)
        ),
      solved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2024 = colDef(
        name = "Percentage point change in solve rate (2019 to 2024)",
        cell = add_plus_sign_percent_point_change,
        )
      )
    )

```



```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### By offense")
```


<span style="color:#E17619"> Orange </span> indicates a solve rate less than the state average across the United States.

<span style="color:#15607A"> Blue </span> indicates a solve rate greater than or equal to the state average across the United States.


```{r}
###################################################################################
### prep df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final for table
###################################################################################

### clean up rates 
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final <-
  df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024 |> 
  ### recode all solved incident data to NA if state %in% states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz
  mutate(
    across(c(solved_rate_aggravated_assault,
             solved_rate_homicide,
             solved_rate_rape,
             solved_rate_robbery),
                ~ if_else(state_name%in%states_to_exclude_from_solved_rate_viz,
                          NA, .)))

### determine median solve rates by violent offense type for state
### we'll use these state averages to develop an agency-level comparison in the table
state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final$solved_rate_aggravated_assault,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_homicide <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final$solved_rate_homicide,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_rape <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final$solved_rate_rape,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)

state_median_solved_rate_robbery <- median(df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final$solved_rate_robbery,
                                                      na.rm=TRUE)


### final prep df for table
df_nibrs_violent_unsolved_by_state_and_type_to_table_2024_final |> 
  mutate(across(c(solved_rate_aggravated_assault,
                  solved_rate_homicide,
                  solved_rate_rape,
                  solved_rate_robbery
                  ), 
                ~ replace(.x, is.nan(.x), NA))
         ) |> 
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("state_name" = "asc"),
    columns = list(
      state_name = colDef(name = "State", minWidth = 250,
                             sticky = "left",
      # Add a right border style to visually distinguish the sticky column
      style = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee"),
      headerStyle = list(borderRight = "1px solid #eee")),
      n_reported_rate_aggravated_assault = colDef(name = "Aggravated Assault Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE), minWidth = 120), 
      solved_rate_aggravated_assault = colDef(
        name = paste0("Aggravated Assault Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>', "(State Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE), 
        minWidth = 120,
        style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_aggravated_assault & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }
        
        ),
      n_reported_rate_homicide = colDef(name = "Homicide Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE), 
                               align = "center",
          ),
      solved_rate_homicide = colDef(
        name = paste0("Homicide Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(State Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_homicide,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        minWidth = 120,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
        align = "center",
       style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_homicide & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_homicide & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),

      n_reported_rate_rape = colDef(name = "Rape Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)),
      solved_rate_rape = colDef(
        name = paste0("Rape Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(State Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_rape,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,
        minWidth = 155,
        align = "left",
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
        style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_rape & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_rape & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    }),
      n_reported_rate_robbery = colDef(name = "Robbery Incidents per 100k",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)), 
      solved_rate_robbery = colDef(
        name = paste0("Robbery Solve Rate",'<br>','<br>',"(State Average: ",scales::percent(state_median_solved_rate_robbery,accuracy=1),")"),
        html = TRUE,  
        minWidth = 120,
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE),
                style = function(value) {
      if (value >= state_median_solved_rate_robbery & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#15607A"
      } else if (value <= state_median_solved_rate_robbery & !is.na(value)) {
        color <- "#E17619"
      } else {
        color <- "black"
      }
      list(color = color, fontWeight = "bold")
    })
    ))
```

:::

Data regarding crime and solve rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr). Violent crime includes homicide, aggravated assault, robbery, and rape. Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total population for a given geography. Solve rates are the number of reported crimes cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below. 

<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("## Trends in law enforcement resources")
```

State expenditures include spending on police, sheriffs, and highway patrols. The charts below also include staffing per 10,000 residents and per 1,000 violent crimes. These factors may show the level of policing a community receives, potentially influencing both crime and solve rates and providing insight into how law enforcement resources impact violent crime.  

::: {.panel-tabset}



```{r}
### clean up df for plotting
asslgf_clean_percent_change <- asslgf_clean_national |> 
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year_resources <- max(asslgf_clean_percent_change$year)
min_year_resources <- min(asslgf_clean_percent_change$year)

pct_change_spending <- scales::percent((asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources],
                                       accuracy = 1)

pct_change_spending_abs_value <- scales::comma(abs((asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / asslgf_clean_percent_change$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars[asslgf_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources])*100,
                                       accuracy = 1)


change_spending_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_spending >= 0 ~ paste0(" increased"),
  pct_change_spending < 0 ~ paste0(" decreased")
  # TRUE ~ " changed"
  )


```

<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### State expenditures")
```


```{r, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "Overall, state spending on law enforcement",change_spending_text_clear, " across the United States", " by ", pct_change_spending_abs_value," percent from ", min_year_resources, " to ",max_year_resources, ", adjusting for inflation." 
))
```


```{r}
to_plot <- asslgf_clean_national |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", "United States", " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Inflation-adjusted expenditure: ", scales::dollar(n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars, 
                                                        accuracy = 1,
                                                        prefix = '$'))
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, n_inflation_adjusted_latest_year_dollars),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("State law enforcement expenditures")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Inflation-adjusted law enforcement expenditures, 2013–2023") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "US Census Bureau Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    title = "",
    labels = list(
      formatter = JS("function() { return '$' + (this.value / 1000000000) + 'B'; }")
    )) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |>
  hc_setup()

```


<br>

```{r}
### clean up ucr_employee_data_national_clean for text output below
ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change <- ucr_employee_data_national_clean |> 
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year_resources <- max(ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year)
min_year_resources <- min(ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year)

pct_change_staffing <- scales::percent((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources],
                                       accuracy = 1)

pct_change_staffing_abs_value <- scales::comma(abs((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_pop_rate_per_10k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources])*100,
                                       accuracy = 1)


change_staffing_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing >= 0 ~ paste0(" increased"),
  pct_change_staffing < 0 ~ paste0(" decreased")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )

```

<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### Officers per capita")
```


```{r, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "The number of officers per 10k residents in jurisdictions across the United States has", change_staffing_text_clear, " by ", pct_change_staffing_abs_value," percent from ", min_year_resources, " to ",max_year_resources,"." 
))
```


```{r }
to_plot <- ucr_employee_data_national_clean |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", "United States", " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Officers per 10k residents: ", scales::comma(officer_pop_rate_per_10k, 
                                                        accuracy = .1))
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$officer_pop_rate_per_10k)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult *2.5

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, officer_pop_rate_per_10k),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Officers per capita")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Officers per 10k residents, 2013–2024") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates<br>FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program Police Employee Data") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
    hc_yAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}")
    ) |>
  hc_setup()

```

<br>

```{r}
### create pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense and change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear
### for text below

pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense <- scales::percent((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources],
                                       accuracy = 1)


pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense_abs_value <- scales::comma(abs((ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==max_year_resources] - ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources]) / ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$officer_per_violent_offense_1k[ucr_employee_data_by_state_clean_percent_change$year==min_year_resources])*100,
                                       accuracy = 1)

change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense >= 0 ~ paste0(" increased"),
  pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense < 0 ~ paste0(" decreased")
  # TRUE ~ " has stayed nearly the same"
  )

```

<br>

```{r, results='asis'}
cat("### Officers per violent offense")
```


```{r, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "The number of officers per 1k violent crimes in jurisdictions across the United States has", change_staffing_per_violent_offense_text_clear, " by ", pct_change_staffing_per_violent_offense_abs_value," percent from ", min_year_resources, " to ",max_year_resources,"." 
))
```


```{r }
to_plot <- ucr_employee_data_national_clean |> 
  mutate(
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", "United States", " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Officers per 1k violent crimes: ", scales::comma(officer_per_violent_offense_1k, 
                                                        accuracy = .1))
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$officer_per_violent_offense_1k)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, officer_per_violent_offense_1k),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Officers per violent offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Officers per 1k violent crimes, 2013–2024") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates<br>FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program Police Employee Data") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |> 
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
    hc_yAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}")
    ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

:::

<br>

```{r, results='asis' }
cat("## Trends in homicide solve rates")
```

The following chart includes homicide solve rates by race and ethnicity, gender, age, weapon type, and number of victims. For more information, see the data and methodology sections below.  

In the United States, homicide solve rates are highest when the victim is White and are lowest when the victim is Black. Homicide solve rates are highest when the victim is a woman and lowest when the victim is a man. 

::: {.panel-tabset}


### Race and ethnicity 

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
### this function will prepare a df for plotting -- grouping by
### varying incident or demographic characteristics
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_national_plot(df = shr_1976_2024_national_race,
                                          var = "victim_1_race_clean") 


### now plot
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_race_clean, 
          clearance_rate),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by race and ethnicity")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2020–2024") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's race and ethnicity is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data (2020-2024)")) |>     # hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```



### Gender

```{r }
### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_national_plot(df = shr_1976_2024_national_gender,
                                          var = "victim_1_sex_clean") 


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_sex_clean, clearance_rate),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by gender")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2020–2024") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's gender is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data (2020-2024)")) |>     
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```



### Age


```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_national_plot(df = shr_1976_2024_national_age,
                                          var = "victim_1_age_bin")


to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_1_age_bin, clearance_rate),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by age")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2020–2024") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis excludes records where a victim's age is unknown<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data (2020-2024)")) |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data") |> 
    # hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()

```


### Weapon 

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_national_plot(df = shr_1976_2024_national_weapon,
                                          var = "offender_1_weapon_clean")  |>
   filter(offender_1_weapon_clean%in%c("Firearm","Personal Weapons (Hands, Feet, Teeth, Etc.)", "Lethal Cutting Instrument (Knife, Ice Pick, Screwdriver, Ax, Etc.)","Drugs/Narcotics/Sleeping Pills", "Blunt Object (E.g., Club/Blackjack/Brass Knuckles)")) |> 
  mutate(
    offender_1_weapon_clean = case_match(
      offender_1_weapon_clean,
      "Firearm" ~ "Firearm",
      "Personal Weapons (Hands, Feet, Teeth, Etc.)" ~ "Personal Weapons",
      "Lethal Cutting Instrument (Knife, Ice Pick, Screwdriver, Ax, Etc.)" ~ "Lethal Cutting Instrument",
      "Drugs/Narcotics/Sleeping Pills" ~ "Drugs/Narcotics",
      "Blunt Object (E.g., Club/Blackjack/Brass Knuckles)" ~ "Blunt Object"
    )
  )

to_plot |> 
  arrange(desc(clearance_rate)) |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(offender_1_weapon_clean, clearance_rate),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by weapon")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2020–2024") |>
  hc_caption(text = list("This analysis includes the most common weapons across states in recent years<br>FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data (2020-2024)")) |> 
  # hc_caption(text = "FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data") |> 
    # hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25)
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```


### Number of victims

```{r }

### clean up df for plot via function
to_plot <- function_shr_grouping_for_national_plot(df = shr_1976_2024_national_single_multiple_victims,
                                          var = "victim_multiple_flag")  |>
  filter(!is.na(victim_multiple_flag)) |> 
### order by single victims and then multiple victims in viz
  mutate(victim_multiple_flag = factor(victim_multiple_flag,
    levels = c("Single Victim","Multiple Victims")))



to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    hcaes(victim_multiple_flag, clearance_rate),
    # color = jr_pal[1],
    # dashStyle = c("solid"),
    # opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide solve rates by number of victims")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "2020–2024") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Supplementary Homicide Report (SHR) Data (2020-2024)") |> 
    # hc_add_theme(hc_theme_jc) |>
    hc_tooltip(formatter = JS("function(){return(this.point.tooltip)}")) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(animation = FALSE),
      accessibility = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
      )
    ) |>
    hc_xAxis(
      title = "",
      labels = list(y = 25),
      categories = levels(to_plot$victim_multiple_flag)
      # reversed = 'true',
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
          title = "",
      labels = list(format = "{value}%")
  ) |>
  hc_setup()


```

:::

<br>

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources and methodology

Data regarding crime and solve rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2014 and 2024 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) and the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2024 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"}, and on the FBI's [Crime Data Explorer](https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/#/pages/home){target="_parent"}. For clearance data, we used [Jacob Kaplan's version of the Offenses Known and Clearances by Arrest](https://www.openicpsr.org/openicpsr/project/100707/version/V21/view) dataset, which includes agency-level data on reported crime and clearances.

The FBI stopped SRS data collection after data year 2020 and required law enforcement agencies to use NIBRS to report crime statistics to the FBI. This change decreased the number of agencies that submitted their crime data and thus reduced the reliability of state estimates in 2021. From 2022 through 2024, the FBI allowed agencies who were unable to submit NIBRS data to submit SRS data again. Ninety-six percent of the U.S. population was covered in the 2024 crime data submissions to the FBI, with most states providing close to complete data. However, the population upon which the estimates were derived decreased by over 10 percent in three states: Florida, Mississippi, and Wyoming, so conclusions about crime changes in those states should be interpreted with additional caution.

Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}.

Solve rates are the number of reported crimes cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. A reported crime incident is [cleared by exceptional means](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/clearances){target='_parent'} if a law enforcement agency is unable to arrest or formally charge a person with a crime, but the agency has met the following conditions:
    
a) Identified the person who committed the crime
b) Gathered enough evidence to support an arrest, make a charge, and turn over the person to the court for prosecution
c) Identified the suspect’s exact location so that the person could be taken into custody immediately
d) Encountered a circumstance outside the control of law enforcement that prohibits the agency from arresting, charging, and prosecuting the person
    
Examples of exceptional clearances include the death of the person who committed the crime; the victim’s refusal to cooperate with the prosecution after the person who committed the crime has been identified; or the denial of extradition.

Data regarding state law enforcement expenditures were downloaded from the U.S. Census Bureau [Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/gov-finances.html){target="_parent"}.

Officer employment analysis is restricted to sworn law enforcement officers and was downloaded from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) Program police employee data files](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/police-employee-data){target="_parent"}.

Data on homicide solve rate trends and case characteristics come from the FBI's [Supplementary Homicide Reporting (SHR) Program](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/expanded-homicide){target="_parent"}.

:::
